message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): URL without path defaults to `/` contract (tick 324)

Added `distributed_http_client_url_without_path_defaults_to_slash` —
pins that a URL with no explicit path (e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:8080`)
produces a `GET / HTTP/1.1` request-target on the wire, not empty or
malformed.

Why this matters: HTTP/1.1 requires a non-empty request-target. Real
distributed callers construct URLs dynamically from host + port pairs
(health checks, event forwarding), and a missing path segment silently
broke many no-path callsites before the client migration. This test
pins the default-to-`/` behavior against regression.

Test asserts the server-received request line starts with
`GET / HTTP/1.` (prefix check tolerates HTTP/1.0 vs HTTP/1.1).

Complements:
- tick 321: explicit path + query-string roundtrip
- tick 316/320/319: body, transport-err, non-2xx semantics

Now covers the three URL path variants that matter in production: root
(this tick), deep path + query (321), and edge failure (320/319).

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 24s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
